titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Black and Blue: Yale Volunteers in the Mississippi Civil Rights Movement Collection
Black and Blue: Yale Volunteers in the Mississippi Civil Rights Movement Collection
titlestmt.author: compiled by Staff of Manuscripts and Archives

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday September 4, 2020 at 07:33

did.unittitle: Black and Blue: Yale Volunteers in the Mississippi Civil Rights Movement, 1963-1965 [research material and senior essay] by Jodi L. Wilgoren, Class of 1992, Yale College
did.abstract: The materials consist of audiotapes and transcripts of interviews, posters, flyers, reminiscences, memoranda, clippings assembled by Jodi L. Wilgoren (Yale 1992) for her senior essay titled Black and Blue: Yale Volunteers in the Mississippi Civil Rights Movement, 1963-1965.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Forms part of Yale Record Group 49 (YRG 49), Papers and projects on Yale.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy for their personal use should consult Copying Services information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright for materials authored or otherwise produced as official business of Yale University is retained by Yale University. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Black and Blue: Yale Volunteers in the Mississippi Civil Rights Movement Collection (RU 98). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Yale University records are arranged and described at the accession level by the creating office. The University Archives creates collection level descriptive records, but typically does no further arrangement and description at the accession level.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The materials consist of audiotapes and transcripts of interviews, posters, flyers, reminiscences, memoranda, clippings assembled by Jodi L. Wilgoren (Yale 1992) for her senior essay titled Black and Blue: Yale Volunteers in the Mississippi Civil Rights Movement, 1963-1965.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The materials are arranged in four sections, Tape Recordings of Interviews Conducted for the Project; Transcripts of Interviews; Senior Essay; Materials Collected During the Project.

did.unittitle: Black and Blue: Yale volunteers in the Mississippi civil rights movement
Tape recordings of interviews conducted for the project

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy for their personal use should consult Copying Services information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site.

Transcripts of interviews conducted for the project

Coffin, William Sloane, Jr. (release on file)
Nielsen, Vigo G., Jr. (release on file)
Wilgoren, Jodi Lynne. "Black and Blue: Yale Volunteers in the Mississippi Civil Rights Movement, 1963-1965." Submitted as senior essay for History 495-6
Materials collected during project [includes posters, handouts, flyers, notes and reminiscences of participants, memorand, and clippings]
